packageRules.description: Hold TypeScript on the 6.x line. TS 7 is the Go-native port: its package exports drop the compiler API ('.' resolves to lib/version.cjs, with the real surface behind './unstable/*'), and @astrojs/check -> @astrojs/language-server -> volar-service-typescript still consumes the old API, so `astro check` would break at runtime. @astrojs/check@0.9.9 (latest, so there is no upgrade path around it) also declares peerDependencies typescript ^5.0.0 || ^6.0.0, which makes a bump to 7 fail `npm ci` with ERESOLVE and take the Pages deploy with it. An allowedVersions ceiling rather than a disable, so 6.x patches still flow. Lift this once @astrojs/check widens its peer range and the Volar stack ports to the unstable/* API.
